codeReview.summary: The transaction() context manager implementation is solid and well-tested. The code correctly implements atomic operations with proper commit/rollback behavior, exception handling, and state management. The implementation follows SQLite best practices and includes comprehensive test coverage (10 tests covering all critical scenarios). The feature fully satisfies the m1-a4 assertion requirements.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the backend-worker skill procedure correctly: wrote tests first (TDD red phase), implemented to make tests pass (green phase), ran validators (pytest), and documented verification steps. The handoff shows proper adherence to the skill's work procedure with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: (1) Read mission/validation files, (2) Examined existing Database class, (3) Wrote 10 comprehensive tests in test_database.py, (4) Implemented transaction() method with _in_transaction flag, (5) Modified execute() to respect transaction state, (6) Ran pytest to verify all tests pass, (7) Committed with descriptive message. Handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure = true, deviations = [].

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows established patterns in the Database class: uses @contextmanager decorator, includes comprehensive docstrings with examples, proper error handling with logger.warning/debug, and raises RuntimeError for misuse. The _in_transaction flag pattern is clean and prevents auto-commits during transactions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: kiro/core/database.py:283-332 - transaction() method follows same style as other Database methods: docstring with Args/Returns/Raises/Example sections, logger usage for debugging, proper exception handling. The _in_transaction flag (line 51) integrates cleanly with execute() method (line 123-124).

summary: Feature m1-f2-transaction-support successfully implements transaction support for the Database class. The transaction() context manager provides atomic operations with automatic commit on success and rollback on exception. Implementation includes proper state management via _in_transaction flag to prevent auto-commits during transactions. All 10 transaction tests pass, covering commit, rollback, multiple operations, nested transactions, isolation, and exception propagation. No regressions detected (all 35 database tests pass). Worker followed TDD procedure correctly. Code quality is high with comprehensive documentation and error handling. Feature fully satisfies m1-a4 assertion.
